title: braya humilis var. interior
intro: Braya humilis var. interior, also known as Arctic Alpine Borage, is a subspecies of the Braya humilis plant, which is a perennial herb native to the Arctic regions. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Braya humilis var. interior include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory issues, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant and protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and has been traditionally used by indigenous communities in the Arctic region to treat various health conditions.
